title: Kubernetes Pod Status Summary
description: Parse the output of 'kubectl get pods' and produce a summary of pod health. Count total pods, running pods, non-running pods, and identify pods with high restart counts. Return a dictionary with total, running, not_running, and high_restarts (list of (name, restart_count) tuples sorted by restart count descending).
constraints: Input is kubectl get pods output; the first line is the header and must be skipped
A pod is 'running' if STATUS column equals 'Running'
RESTARTS column is an integer; pods with restarts > restart_threshold are flagged
high_restarts list is sorted by restart count descending
Return zeros and empty list for header-only input

examples.input:
NAME                    READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
web-7d9f8b-xkr2p         1/1     Running          2   1d
api-5c8d4f-lmn3q         0/1     Pending          0   2h
db-6e7f9g-pqr4s          1/1     Running         12   3d
worker-8h9i0j-tuv5w      0/1     Error            7   5h
examples.explanation: 4 pods total. 2 are Running. 2 are not (Pending, Error). db and worker exceed the restart threshold of 5, sorted by count descending.

starter_code.python:
def pod_summary(kubectl_output: str, restart_threshold: int = 5) -> dict:
    pass

test_cases.description: 4 pods: 2 running, 2 not running, 2 high restart counts
test_cases.input:
NAME                    READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
web-7d9f8b-xkr2p         1/1     Running          2   1d
api-5c8d4f-lmn3q         0/1     Pending          0   2h
db-6e7f9g-pqr4s          1/1     Running         12   3d
worker-8h9i0j-tuv5w      0/1     Error            7   5h

test_cases.description: All pods running with low restarts
test_cases.input:
NAME     READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
pod-1      1/1     Running          0   1h
pod-2      1/1     Running          1   2h

test_cases.description: Header only (no pods) returns all zeros
